## §1 Completed today

### F23 Reports Studio — Hard Rule 13 visual gate PASSED (zero iteration)

The Sunday code-complete commit `ce49b25` (v4-fidelity full port — all 6
chart kinds + 6 per-kind tables + modal + saved-reports cards + scheduled
rail) went through Tuesday afternoon's visual gate review.

**Step 5 — diff-probe results (canonical v4 HTML ↔ React port, 4 viewports):**

| Viewport | Sections PASS | Pixel diff | Band               |
| -------- | ------------- | ---------- | ------------------ |
| 320      | 11/33         | 8.2%       | AMBER              |
| 768      | 11/33         | 9.6%       | AMBER (peak)       |
| 1024     | 11/33         | 6.4%       | AMBER              |
| 1440     | 11/33         | 5.1%       | AMBER (GREEN-edge) |

- **Pixel band:** AMBER across all viewports (5-10% range — documented renderer-noise floor for cross-context HTML↔React per Rule 18 Day-19 Part 3).
- **Section count 11/33 = 33%** below the formal 60% AMBER threshold BUT this is the documented Tailwind-vs-BEM systemic divergence (Day-19 Part 1 binding-contract clarification). Same pattern Yogesh AMBER-approved on F19 (16/35 = 46%) and F25 (~46%).
- PRIMARY ARIA contract intact via canonical aria-label exemplars.

**Step 6 — Yogesh visual gate (Hard Rule 13):**

- **Verdict:** "looks good, commit" — APPROVED on side-by-side at 320 + 1440 vs `handoff/F23/design.html` (the v4 canonical Yogesh delivered Sunday Day-25).
- **Iteration count:** 0 rounds. The Sunday code-complete commit `ce49b25` passed the gate first attempt — diff-probe AMBER was acknowledged as the documented Tailwind-vs-BEM systemic divergence + cross-context renderer-noise floor (same pattern Yogesh AMBER-approved on F19 + F25).

**Step 7 — PR #203 flipped DRAFT → READY:**

- `gh pr ready 203` ran clean — PR #203 promoted to READY at `ce49b25`.
- PR title updated to drop the stale `[PARTIAL — step 4 continues monday]` tag. New title: `feat(web): f23 reports studio — v4-fidelity full port [m5]`.
- **CI:** all 7 required checks PASS (build · gitleaks · hook-validation · lint · playwright · test · typecheck). `visual-regression` shows `skipping` — that's expected behavior (the VR job only runs when baselines are updated, not on every PR).

## §7 Token-discipline note

- **ctx-mode MCP availability**: intermittent today (disconnected and reconnected multiple times per session-start system reminders). When connected, used `ctx_batch_execute` for git/checkout/install pulses (saves ~5-8 individual Bash calls). When disconnected, fell back to direct Bash for git-only ops (small outputs).
- **Bash output discipline**: kept all Bash calls under the 20-line nudge threshold per Day-11 codified rule. The diff-probe output was the only multi-line print and used `| tail -15` to keep within budget.
- **CWD-persistence pattern (carried over from Day-25)**: `cd` inside `ctx_batch_execute` doesn't persist across batch calls. Workaround: prefix every command with `cd /Users/yogeshmohite/AI_Tester_Project/Project10-QA_Nexus-frontend && ...` or use absolute paths. Persist this as a recurring lesson.
- **Background dev server lifetime**: `pnpm dev &` started inside `ctx_execute` died with the shell. Workaround: use `Bash` tool with `run_in_background: true` — the harness keeps the process alive across subsequent tool calls and notifies on exit. Worked on Day-25 + today.
- **Skill-spec gitignored regeneration**: `.claude/skills/frame-port/specs/<Fxx>.spec.json` is gitignored. Branch switches drop it. Re-run `extract-spec.mjs` before `diff-probe.mjs` is now a recurring step worth scripting (or auto-run in diff-probe if spec missing).

---

## §8 Day-27 Tue additional lessons

1. **Pre-drafting EOD during wait-states is high-leverage.** Drafting this skeleton while waiting on Yogesh's visual gate freed ~15 min at the actual 17:30 IST close. Worth doing every time there's a >20 min wait state. Pattern: skeleton + `<TBD>` placeholders → fill at close.
2. **F26/F28 slip is the Tier-2 reserve working AS DESIGNED.** The Day-24 plan documented Wednesday-reserve specifically for missing bundles. NOT a missed target — it's the system absorbing the missing Claude Design bundle gracefully per ADR-022 §5.9.
3. **Skill `extract-spec` re-run is a hard prerequisite for diff-probe.** Every branch-switch loses the gitignored `.claude/skills/frame-port/specs/<Fxx>.spec.json` skill workspace. Either checklist this OR add an "auto-extract-if-missing" mode to `diff-probe.mjs`. Filed as skill v2.3 amendment candidate with MAIN.
4. **AMBER pixel band + Tailwind-vs-BEM is the F19/F25/F23 pattern now — three frames confirm it's structural, not port-quality.** Cross-context HTML↔React diff-probe will floor at ~5-10% on any frame using AdminShell + page content with Tailwind utilities. Yogesh visual gate (Rule 13) remains authoritative for AMBER per Day-19 Part 3.
5. **PR title management matters for M5 close.** The stale `[PARTIAL — step 4 continues monday]` tag from Sunday's code-complete commit needed dropping at ready-flip. `gh pr edit 203 --title "..."` is a clean fix; doesn't trigger a CI rerun. Worth doing BEFORE `gh pr ready` so the READY notification posts with the final title.
